title: Knowledge-OS Manifest
description: The C->B contract: a single declarative description of an agent-navigable Drive that fully parameterizes every deterministic (B) tool. Layered into a company_profile (invariants shared across teammates, seeded once) and a person_profile (focus-specific to one person, partly populated by the focus-detector C module). Grounded in the MetaOptics OneDrive instance.

manifest_version.description: Schema version of this manifest document, e.g. '0.1'.

company_profile.description: Company-INVARIANT slots: shared across every teammate's instance and seeded from one company profile (never a live central store). Re-derived only when standing up a different company.

domain.description: Primary email/web domain, e.g. 'metaoptics.sg'. Used to recognise internal vs external email senders.

taxonomy.description: Top-level folder tiers + the deriveCategory rule. Parameterizes the graph indexer's category derivation and the placement-rule engine.

projects_root.description: The single top-level folder that holds the project/entity tiers (project_tiers live one segment under it). The reference instance (MetaOptics) uses '__Projects'. Read by the entity-registry generator (kb-entities) and the focus detector (kb-focus) to build their company-deriving regexes instead of hardcoding the hub name — a Drive whose hub is named differently (e.g. 'Projects') still derives companies.

project_tiers.description: The top-level __Projects/ tiers (folder + purpose), from README_Folder_Guidelines.md 'Tier definitions'.

naming_convention.description: Optional folder-naming rule, e.g. the YYYYMMDD date-prefix scheme for Aquisition.

entity_card.description: Whether folders in this tier are entity/relationship cards the card extractor (kb-extract) should surface as project tiles (true for relationship tiers like Aquisition/Internal/Collaboration/Archive), vs record-only tiers whose contents are documents not entities (false for Sales/Documentation). Lets kb-extract decide card-bearing tiers from the manifest instead of a hardcoded tier list.

category_rules.description: Ordered first-match rules mapping a root-relative path to a graph category (mirrors mot-tools.js deriveCategory). Lifecycle folders (_superseded/legacy/Archive) match before location.

match.description: A regex (path-segment) or exact path the rule tests against the root-relative path.

scope.description: For second_segment_of rules: the first segment this rule applies under (e.g. '__Projects').

subfolder_convention.description: Canonical purpose-named subfolder names attachments port into (README Rule 4) — the ingest/port skill's allowed destinations. No raw landing zone.

non_card_subfolders.description: Deep-technical working subfolder names that hold engineering/working documents, NOT entity cards — even inside an entity_card:true tier. The card extractor (kb-extract) does NOT treat an Overview/CLAUDE.md under one of these as a separate project tile (e.g. Development, Analysis, Testing, 'Measurement setup'). Complements project_tiers[].entity_card: the tier flag says which tiers card at all; this list carves card-free working zones out of those tiers.

vocab.description: Controlled vocabularies the B-tools and skills enforce. The single source of truth for tier scale, phase enum, verticals, edge-types, node_kinds, status, and supply-chain roles.

labels.description: Map of tier number -> short label.

verticals.description: Product lanes (frontmatter `vertical`; also the Sync verticals view and the __MOT folder tree top level).

edge_types.description: Controlled `references[].type` vocabulary (Reference_Graph_Schema §3). Drives the ref validator's invalid/legacy classification.

legacy.description: Discouraged synonyms still present in old files; reported as low-severity, not invalid.

provenance.description: Document-flow types whose dead target under the temp archive is provenance, not drift.

supply_chain_roles.description: Observed values of `supply_chain_role`. NOTE: currently UNCONTROLLED in MOT (free-form). Listed so a B-tool can normalise; flag drift against this set. Must cover the FULL live spread, including the off-enum live values surfaced by the audit (`none`, `software_platform`, `research-partner`), not just the canonical roles. KNOWN TODO: this set still contains synonym pairs that should be de-duped to one canonical each (`researcher`/`research`, `supplier`/`vendor`) — left in until a normalisation map is added so a B-tool can recognise every live value without false-flagging it as drift.

document_kinds.description: The document-genre vocabulary the focus detector (kb-focus) uses to classify a node by its path. Generalizes kb-focus's formerly-hardcoded docKindDefs (Testing/analysis, Meeting_Records, the weekly sync filename, Standards, __Literature) into manifest-declared kinds so a different company's genres are visible. Each entry pairs a stable key + human label with one or more regex path-patterns; a node is counted under a kind if ANY pattern matches its root-relative id. `literature` is special-cased by key (excluded from focus genres) so name it `literature` to preserve that behaviour.

key.description: Stable machine key, e.g. 'meeting_records'. Used for counts and the literature special-case.

label.description: Human label shown in focus output, e.g. 'meeting records'.

path_patterns.description: Regex strings tested against a node's root-relative id (forward-slash). A node matches the kind if ANY pattern matches.

frontmatter_schema.description: Node-record field contract: which frontmatter keys are required/optional on a content node, plus the TL;DR-head key set the card extractor parses.

required_fields.description: Minimum keys every graph-registered .md must carry (root CLAUDE.md 'Graph registration').

optional_fields.description: Recognised optional node fields the indexer/extractor read (tier, phase, vertical, status, context, valid_as_of, node_kind, supply_chain_role, agent_read, etc.).

tldr_keys.description: The `## TL;DR` bullet keys (Quality_Standards.md 'TL;DR block'). NOTE: the extractor (kb-extract / extractProjects) parses ALL bold-key bullets generically (every `**Key:** value` line, ~70 keys in the wild), so this is NOT an allow-list — `canonical` is only the REQUIRED/validated subset plus the freshness anchor. A bullet key absent from `canonical` is still extracted; it is just not required or freshness-anchored.

canonical.description: The REQUIRED/validated bullet keys, in order. The minimum subset every Overview TL;DR must carry — and the keys card slots are guaranteed. NOT an allow-list: the extractor still reads any other bold-key bullet generically.

date_anchored_key.description: Which TL;DR key must lead with an ISO date for the freshness dot.

date_anchored_synonyms.description: Alternate bold-key labels that ALSO carry the date-anchored freshness value (treated as equivalent to `date_anchored_key` for the freshness dot), e.g. an Overview that leads with 'Last engagement' instead of the canonical key. Lets the extractor anchor freshness without forcing every Overview onto one label.

avoid_read_marker.description: The frontmatter convention that flags large files agents should not full-read.

extractor_field.description: Frontmatter key naming the extractor to use instead, e.g. 'agent_read_extractor'.

context_registry.description: Confusable-workstream registry (Contexts.md): each context names its owning current-position file and its related-but-different siblings with a mandatory difference note. The guardrail that stops lookalike workstreams overwriting each other. Company-invariant because the confusables are about the company's projects.

owner.description: Root-relative path to the current-position file. null is a DELIBERATE state for a sync-owned / not-yet-active context (no single current-position home) — tooling treats it as intentional, not a broken lookup.

owner_note.description: For owner:null contexts, where the consolidated state actually lives (e.g. 'weekly sync OPERATIONS section').

related.description: Sibling contexts and HOW they differ (the load-bearing note).

difference.description: Why this sibling must never bleed into the context.

entity_registry.description: Seed people->roles registry (root CLAUDE.md Key People). The single-source-of-truth MOT never built as a standalone artifact; seeded here. Companies are derivable from __Projects/ folders, so only the people seed is mandatory.

excludes.description: Skip-lists for the walker/indexer. THE SINGLE SOURCE: excludes.json `dirs`. Flagged here because MOT currently hand-mirrors this list across server.js, mot-tools.js (both read the JSON) AND the C++ walker SKIP_DIRS (cannot read JSON) — a drift risk. The manifest should be the one source all tools read.

dirs.description: Directory basenames never entered into the graph/tree/catalogs.

skip_exts.description: WALKER/SERVER-SCOPED. File extensions ignored by the catalog walker + dashboard server. The graph indexer (kb-index / mot-tools.js graph) does NOT apply these — it indexes by frontmatter, not extension. Lives in the C++ walker today.

skip_names.description: WALKER/SERVER-SCOPED. Exact/glob filenames the catalog walker + dashboard server skip (e.g. _catalog.md folder listings, machine-suffixed *-HOST.md). The graph INDEXER does NOT consult this list (mot-tools.js buildGraphIndex indexes _catalog.md); the indexer's own conflict-file exclusion is `conflict_pattern`. GAP-1 resolution: one list cannot parameterize two tools with different rules, so name lists are tool-scoped — skip_names is walker/server, conflict_pattern is indexer.

conflict_pattern.description: INDEXER-SCOPED. A regex matched against a node's basename (or root-relative path); files that match are the only names the graph indexer (kb-index / mot-tools.js buildGraphIndex) excludes — i.e. machine-suffixed merge-conflict files like `-METAOPTICS<n>.md`. Distinct from skip_names (which is walker/server-scoped and the indexer ignores), so the manifest can say the walker skips _catalog.md while the indexer still indexes it.

exclude_path_patterns.description: Raw-artifact subtree patterns excluded from the graph (e.g. transcript/frames folders, __temp/Junk).

catalog_profile.description: WALKER-SCOPED file classification + filtering for the per-folder catalog generator (kb-walk, the generalized mot-walker). Closes the SSOT risk that these constants lived ONLY in the C++ walker (`mot-walker.cpp` SKIP_EXTS / EXT_MAP / SKIP_NAMES) and had no manifest home — so kb-walk had to fall back to a hardcoded BUILTIN mirror. With this section present, kb-walk is a 100% pure function of the manifest (no BUILTIN fallback). Distinct from `excludes`: `excludes.dirs` prunes whole DIRECTORIES from every tool; this section governs how the WALKER lists/classifies the FILES inside a folder it does enter.

skip_exts.description: Lowercased file extensions (leading dot) the catalog walker omits from a folder's `_catalog.md` — data/binary/system junk a catalog of 'real' content shouldn't list. Checked BEFORE classification (an ext here is dropped even if ext_classification also maps it). Source of record: mot-walker.cpp SKIP_EXTS.

ext_classification.description: Extension -> file-type classifier the walker uses to label a listed file (machine `type` + human `type_label`). Drives the catalog's per-file Type column and the folder description's type-label summary. Source of record: mot-walker.cpp EXT_MAP. An extension absent from this map falls back to `default_type`.

type_label.description: Human label shown in the catalog, e.g. 'PDF', 'Word', 'Excel'.

default_type.description: Fallback classification for an extension not in ext_classification (mot-walker.cpp returns {'document','File'}).

skip_exact_names.description: Exact filenames (OS/system junk with no/uncommon extension) the catalog walker omits — e.g. .DS_Store, Thumbs.db, desktop.ini. Distinct from excludes.skip_names (walker name-GLOBS like `_catalog.md`/`*-HOST.md`): this is the exact-match OS-junk set. Source of record: mot-walker.cpp SKIP_NAMES.

catalog_filename.description: The catalog filename the walker emits and skips-as-self (mot-walker.cpp CATALOG_FILENAME). Default '_catalog.md'.

walker_version.description: The `walker_version` stamped into generated catalog frontmatter (mot-walker.cpp emits 1). Bump on a catalog-format change.

max_depth.description: Maximum recursion depth the walker descends (the dashboard server caps at 8).

raw_archive_roots.description: Top-level roots holding the RAW source archive (un-curated inbound material: the flat email/recordings dump) whose own derived `.md` summaries are the curated record. The provenance auditor (kb-audit) reads this EXPLICITLY to decide that a dead reference whose target lives under one of these roots is provenance (a document-flow source link to material that was archived), not drift — instead of deriving the temp root implicitly from input_adapters. Usually ['__temp'].

scan_roots.description: Top-level roots the frontmatter indexer walks (mot-tools.js SCAN_ROOTS). The server variant walks the whole Drive from root; this is the lean agent-index subset.

migration_profile.description: MIGRATION-KIT-SCOPED config: parameterizes the reversible database-first migration tools (migration/inventory.mjs, plan-renames.mjs, apply-moves.mjs). OPTIONAL — a Drive that is already organized never needs it; the tools fall back to documented defaults + the existing taxonomy/catalog_profile sections when it is absent. Generalizes MOT's one-off __Literature/_migration constants (the PDF inventory scope, the YYYY_Venue_Authors_Title filename grammar, the Papers/Patents/_superseded target layout) into manifest slots so the SAME tools migrate a different Drive by swapping values here.

scan_roots.description: Roots the migration INVENTORY walks (often a single messy folder being restructured, e.g. ['__Literature']). Falls back to company_profile.scan_roots. Distinct from the indexer's scan_roots because a migration usually targets ONE subtree, not the whole agent index.

include_exts.description: If set, the inventory lists ONLY these lowercased extensions (the literature kit inventoried only '.pdf'). Absent ⇒ every file (minus catalog_profile.skip_exts / skip_exact_names). The {include-exts} slot.

near_dup_normalize_strip.description: Regex of characters stripped when normalizing a filename for same-name/near-duplicate grouping (default whitespace + separators + brackets/quotes). Mirrors inventory.py norm_name. The {near-dup-normalize} slot.

near_dup_tail_drop.description: Regex of trailing tokens dropped after normalize (e.g. 'copy', 'kopie', a trailing digit) so 'paper(1)' groups with 'paper'. Mirrors inventory.py.

target_layout.description: Where the plan routes survivors, kind-variants, surplus duplicates, and supplements. The {target-layout} slot family. Generalizes the literature kit's Papers/ + Patents/ + _superseded/ + _SI scheme.

primary.description: Flat survivor folder for the default kind, e.g. 'Papers'. {primary-folder}

variants.description: Map of file kind -> its own target folder (e.g. {"patent":"Patents"}). {variant-folders}

superseded.description: Sink folder for surplus duplicate copies (NEVER deleted; old relative path mirrored under it). Default '_superseded'. {superseded-sink}

supplement_suffix.description: Suffix appended to a main file's basename for a supplement that gets NO own graph node (e.g. '_SI'). {supplement-suffix}

filename_template.description: The rename grammar the planner applies per survivor. The {filename-template} slot. Generalizes the literature kit's 'YYYY_JournalAbbrev_FirstAuthor_LastAuthor_Short-Title' rule into ordered, manifest-declared components.

components.description: Metadata field names joined IN ORDER to form the basename (e.g. ['year','venue','first_author','last_author','title']). A field absent from a file's resolved metadata is skipped.

title_words.description: Max words kept from the title component.

max_filename_len.description: Hard cap on the basename length (literature kit used 120 to stay under the OneDrive path limit).

ascii_fold.description: Fold diacritics to ASCII (é→e) so filenames are portable.

taxonomy_source.description: Which manifest taxonomy section labels a file's `category` when resolved metadata lacks one. Default 'category_rules' (reuse company_profile.taxonomy.category_rules path→category mapping). The {taxonomy-source} slot.

metadata_sources.description: Ordered confidence ladder of metadata provenance, strongest first (e.g. ['bib','pdf-embedded','crossref','first-page','filename','unresolved']). A file resolved from a source ranked weaker than `min_confident_source` is flagged needs_review so the C rung confirms it before gate 2. The {metadata-ladder} slot.

min_confident_source.description: The weakest metadata source still treated as confident (no review flag). Must appear in metadata_sources. Default 'crossref'.

brand.description: Visual + output identity for the dashboard theme and the PDF renderer.

fonts.description: Font stack used in the PDF print CSS.

pdf_footer.description: PDF footer template/text (sync-report-pdf.js), with placeholders for company + date.

web_qa_target.description: Public site URL the web-qa skill drives.

input_adapters.description: Source-grammar adapters for ingest skills: email archive filename grammar + junk patterns, and the meeting-transcription backend.

email.description: Email archive adapter (Email_Archive_Format.md). MOT uses a FLAT Outlook export; the framework prefers a proper mail API but supports flat-export as a fallback.

junk_patterns.description: Filename globs that are signature logos / Outlook artifacts (cleanup targets).

backend.description: Named pipeline/tool that produces transcripts (e.g. the external 'MOTorga' pipeline / ffmpeg pre-step). MOT has no in-repo /transcribe skill.

cadence.description: Sync period + the freeze/draft boundary the ops-sync skill runs on.

publish_day.description: Day the sync is frozen/published, e.g. 'Tuesday'.

model.description: Prep model, e.g. 'rolling-prep: prior week frozen, next-week draft accumulates'.

pdf_render_schedule.description: Scheduled PDF render, e.g. 'Tuesday 07:00 (MOT Weekly Sync PDF task)'.

storage_profile.description: Where the Drive lives, which toggles churn/lock guards in the B-tools.

root.description: Absolute Drive root (the only absolute path B-tools need). Person/instance overridable but seeded company-wide for the reference machine.

protected_root_markers.description: Marker substrings identifying a LIVE / protected Drive the migration kit must never mutate. If the manifest's resolved root contains any of these, the migration executor (apply-moves.mjs) HARD-REFUSES --apply/--rollback unless the operator sets MIGRATION_TARGET_IS_NOT_PROTECTED=1 AND passes --i-understand. The reference instance uses ['OneDrive - MetaOptics']; an unprotected target Drive leaves this empty (`[]`) so a real migration can run.

churn_guards.description: Prefer incremental writes + content-equality no-op + tmp-rename swaps.

person_profile.description: Per-PERSON focus slots. Seeded from company_profile, then ADAPTED to the person's detected focus. Fields marked focus_detected:true are populated by the focus-detector C module from what dominates the person's Drive; the rest are identity/config.

role.description: Job role, the prior for focus inference.

root_override.description: Absolute Drive root for THIS person's machine if it differs from the company seed. Optional.

focus.description: The detected focus profile — what dominates this person's work. Populated by the focus-detector C module (FOCUS-DETECTED).

focus_verticals.description: FOCUS-DETECTED. Verticals (subset of company vocab.verticals) this person concentrates on.

focus_tiers.description: FOCUS-DETECTED. Tier numbers this person actively works (e.g. [1,2]).

focus_contexts.description: FOCUS-DETECTED. Context tags (subset of company context_registry) central to this person.

focus_entities.description: FOCUS-DETECTED. Company/partner Overview paths this person engages most (the accounts that dominate their Drive).

focus_document_kinds.description: FOCUS-DETECTED. Document genres this person produces/consumes most (e.g. test reports, optical analysis, meeting records).

extra_entities.description: FOCUS-DETECTED. Focus-specific entities this person tracks that the company seed lacks (a CFO's banks/auditors; a VP-Systems' instrument vendors). Extends — never overwrites — the company entity_registry.

extra_taxonomy.description: FOCUS-DETECTED. Focus-specific taxonomy extensions (categories/subfolders the person needs that the company seed lacks). Usually empty for a person whose work fits the shared tiers.

cadence_override.description: Per-person cadence deviations from the company sync cadence, if any (optional). Most teammates inherit the company cadence.
